title: Zerna indurata
taxonomy: Zerna indurata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae, which includes grasses. It belongs to the tribe Bromeae and is closely related to other bromegrass species. Zerna indurata is native to parts of North America and is often found in dry to moist areas with well-drained soils. The plant is known to be a perennial or annual, depending on the specific conditions it grows in. Its classification is also sometimes associated with the genus Bromus, which suggests a close taxonomic relationship with other bromegrass species.
